mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which depresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which amplifies equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which signals near-term economic momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during supply-constrained regimes where industrial output rises but margins compress, resulting in negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally despite inflation spikes, or the signal shows no statistical separation from baseline returns over a 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth decouples from equity performance during periods of wage-price spirals, yielding negative forward returns despite consistent employment gains.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend indicates robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in economic strength.
failure_condition: GDP surprises fail to move equity prices when growth is driven by inventory accumulation rather than final sales, resulting in flat or negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment signals a synchronized supply-side recovery and labor market tightening, which boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal breaks down when industrial output rises due to automation while unemployment remains structurally high, causing equity returns to underperform baseline.
